sentence_reviews.text: Paired views or examples define similarity signals that shape a representation space.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: CPC abstract lines 42-44; CLIP abstract lines 42-43.
sentence_reviews.notes: Both CPC and CLIP use paired or contrasting examples to learn representations through similarity/contrast signals.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Contrastive and mutual-information objectives is to connect Contrastive learning, Information theory to downstream capabilities including Multimodal foundation models, Dense vector retrieval.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: CPC abstract lines 42-44; CLIP abstract lines 42-44.
sentence_reviews.notes: Sources support contrastive representation learning and multimodal image-text pretraining; dense retrieval is inferred from representation similarity.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: CLIP abstract lines 42-44 describes pretraining on 400M image-text pairs and transfer to downstream tasks.
sentence_reviews.notes: CLIP is direct evidence for contrastive pretraining of a multimodal foundation-style model.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: CLIP abstract lines 42-43 describes predicting which caption goes with which image and learned representations.
sentence_reviews.notes: Dense retrieval follows from learned vector similarity, but the abstract does not directly name dense retrieval.

sentence_reviews.text: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Contrastive learning, Information theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: CPC abstract lines 42-44 describes a probabilistic contrastive loss and tractability via negative sampling.
sentence_reviews.notes: The source supports tractable contrastive loss and representation learning; it does not directly state the surrogate-objective failure mode.

sentence_reviews.text: Contrastive and mutual-information objectives uses Contrastive learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.notes: Contrastive objectives are the objective form used in contrastive learning.

sentence_reviews.text: Contrastive and mutual-information objectives uses Information theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: CPC abstract lines 42-44 describes capturing information useful for predicting future samples; title and framing relate CPC to representation learning, with mutual-information objective connection in the paper.
sentence_reviews.notes: Mutual information is information-theoretic, but the inspected abstract only indirectly supports the dependency.

sentence_reviews.text: Contrastive and mutual-information objectives uses Embeddings & distributed representations as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: CPC abstract lines 42-44; CLIP abstract lines 42-43.
sentence_reviews.notes: The objectives shape latent or vector representation spaces.

edge_reviews.notes: Contrastive learning is intrinsic to contrastive objectives.
Mutual-information variants depend on information theory; contrastive objectives more broadly need not be explicitly information-theoretic.
Representation spaces are directly central to CPC and CLIP-style contrastive objectives.
